items.mistake: Treating PDF f(x) as P(X = x) for continuous random variables
items.fix: Use f(x) only inside integrals: P(a ≤ X ≤ b) = ∫_a^b f(x) dx; for a single point, P(X = x) = 0.
items.why: Continuous RVs assign zero probability to any exact value; the PDF is a rate of probability per unit x, not a point probability.
items.example: For X ~ Uniform(0, 0.5), f(0.1) = 2 but P(X = 0.1) = 0; P(0 ≤ X ≤ 0.5) = 1 because the integral over the support is 1.

items.mistake: PMF values that do not sum to 1 after manual calculation or model output
items.fix: Verify Σ p(x) = 1 over the full support; renormalize or fix the model if softmax/sigmoid outputs are miscalibrated.
items.why: A function that does not sum (or integrate) to 1 is not a valid probability distribution, so downstream expectations and losses are wrong.
items.example: A three-class classifier outputs [0.5, 0.3, 0.1] summing to 0.9—apply softmax correctly or check for numerical underflow before computing cross-entropy.

items.mistake: Using the wrong CDF tail for 'greater than' events
items.fix: P(X > x) = 1 − F(x) for continuous X; for discrete X, check whether the boundary x is included in P(X ≤ x).
items.why: Off-by-one and boundary inclusion errors flip tail probabilities, breaking SLA estimates and hypothesis tests.
items.example: For discrete X with PMF p(1)=0.2, p(2)=0.5, p(3)=0.3: P(X > 2) = p(3) = 0.3, not 1 − F(2) if F(2) includes P(X ≤ 2) = 0.7.
